_description: Cooling tower configurations for heat rejection to ambient

_units.fan_UA_W_K: W/K - Effective air-side heat transfer coefficient
_units.evap_fraction: dimensionless - Fraction of heat rejected via evaporation (0-1)
_units.latent_heat_J_kg: J/kg - Latent heat of vaporization of water

_sizing_notes.fan_UA_sizing: fan_UA determines heat rejection capacity. Q_reject = fan_UA × (T_basin - T_effective). Size so that at design load, T_basin stays reasonable (30-40°C). Formula: fan_UA = Q_design / (T_basin_max - T_ambient).
_sizing_notes.basin_capacitance: Larger basin_C provides thermal buffering and stability. Rule: basin_C > 10 × Q_design × dt to avoid large temperature swings. For 20kW load with dt=5s: basin_C > 1,000,000 J/K.
_sizing_notes.evap_fraction: Higher evap_fraction = better cooling (lower T_basin) but more water use. Typical range: 0.6-0.9 for wet cooling, 0 for dry cooling.
_sizing_notes.water_consumption: WUE ≈ evap_fraction × Q / (latent_heat × E_IT). For 75% evap at 20kW: ~6 L/hr water consumption.
